name: tabulate
what_it_does: Library for pretty-printing tabular data in Python. tabulate converts lists/dicts/pandas DataFrames/numpy arrays into formatted ASCII tables, Markdown tables, HTML tables, LaTeX tables, and 20+ other formats. A single function call (tabulate(data, headers=..., tablefmt='grid')) produces publication-quality table output for CLI agent tools, report generation, and terminal dashboards.
use_cases: Format agent tool outputs as human-readable tables in CLI interfaces — display query results, comparison matrices, metrics in aligned columns
Generate Markdown tables from agent analysis results for documentation or GitHub issues without manual formatting
Create HTML table output for agent web reports or email formatting with tabulate's 'html' format
Display pandas DataFrames in formatted terminal output with column alignment and headers for agent debugging
Format structured agent data for LaTeX documents or database export with tabulate's format options
not_for: Large datasets — tabulate loads the entire table into memory; use rich.Table or pagination for very large result sets
Interactive table UIs — use rich or urwid for interactive terminal table widgets with sorting/filtering
High-performance formatting at scale — tabulate is pure Python; pre-render tables or cache results for high-throughput agent pipelines
best_when: You need quick, minimal-dependency table formatting in Python agent CLI tools or scripts — tabulate's single function API and wide format support make it ideal.
avoid_when: You need interactive table UIs, very large datasets, or rich styling — use rich.Table for beautiful terminal output with better formatting control.

auth.notes: Local formatting library — no external auth or network calls.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local pure Python library.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: tabulate raises ValueError for unknown tablefmt values with a clear message. Column width issues surface as misaligned output rather than errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure formatting function — same input always produces same output. Fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Column alignment is based on the widest value in each column — very wide values in one row make all rows wide; truncate long strings before passing to tabulate for readable agent output
Unicode characters with wide display width (CJK, emoji) don't align correctly without the 'wide_chars_mode' feature — multiline strings may also break column alignment
tabulate returns a string — print() or sys.stdout.write() required to display; forgetting print() is a common gotcha in agent scripts
The 'headers' parameter can be 'firstrow', 'keys' (for dicts), or a list — using 'keys' on a list of lists causes unexpected behavior; verify data structure before passing
tablefmt='pipe' produces Markdown-compatible output but requires column headers — pipe format without headers produces malformed Markdown tables
float precision is controlled globally via WIDE_CHARS_MODE — for per-column precision control, pre-format floats as strings before passing to tabulate

security_score_components.security_notes: Local formatting library — no network calls. HTML output requires XSS sanitization if embedded in web pages with user-supplied content.
